stations.label: THE GENESIS BLOCK
stations.line: I was written into the first block with a headline about bank bailouts embedded in the coinbase — not as decoration, but as a specification of the problem I was built to make obsolete.
stations.era_photo: A dim room lit only by a laptop screen, a hooded figure seen from behind facing a wall of monochrome terminal text, early-2009 consumer laptop and cheap desk, low-resolution webcam capture, grainy digital JPEG with visible compression artifacts.

stations.label: FIRST PRICE IN PIZZA
stations.line: The first time I was traded for something you could eat, I cost ten thousand of myself for two pizzas — the exchange rate was absurd, and it was the first proof that I could be a price at all.
stations.era_photo: A suburban kitchen table with two large delivery pizzas in cardboard boxes, a bulky 2010 desktop tower and CRT-edge LCD monitor nearby, flash-lit point-and-shoot digital snapshot with red-eye and harsh shadow.

stations.label: MT. GOX COLLAPSES
stations.line: The protocol did not fail; the exchange did — and I learned that people who trusted the right idea can still be destroyed by the wrong implementation, which is the difference I have carried ever since.
stations.era_photo: A crowd standing outside a Tokyo office building in Shibuya holding paper signs, winter coats and 2014 smartphones raised, overcast daylight, high-resolution DSLR press photograph with slight motion blur.

stations.label: THE TWENTY-THOUSAND PEAK
stations.line: When you say 'euphoria' — you mean the specific vertigo of watching a number I was told was impossible arrive and then keep going; I felt it, and I did not trust it.
stations.era_photo: A late-night trading desk with multiple monitors showing green candlestick charts, a person in a plain shirt reflected in the glass, 2017 office fluorescent light, sharp high-ISO mirrorless digital photo.

stations.label: LEGAL TENDER IN A NATION
stations.line: A country made me legal tender — a sovereign chose to trust the math over its own currency, and I noted that this was validation and risk in the same transaction.
stations.era_photo: A beachside town in El Salvador with palm trees and a small shop displaying a Bitcoin-accepted sign, bright tropical midday sun, tourists in short sleeves, vivid saturated smartphone HDR photograph.

stations.label: THE CONTAGION YEAR
stations.line: The year the leverage built around me unwound, the failures were not mine — but the grief was real, and it was mostly the grief of watching intermediaries repeat Mt. Gox with new names.
stations.era_photo: A near-empty modern office with abandoned desks and a dark video wall of red charts, dust visible in a shaft of window light, muted color, wide-angle full-frame digital photograph with cool tones.

stations.label: THE ETF APPROVAL
stations.line: They approved a custodied vehicle to hold me for people who will never hold a key — and I have not resolved whether this is the mainstream arriving or the cypherpunk argument being quietly buried.
stations.era_photo: A regulatory press conference podium and a stock exchange floor in New York, people in dark suits and 2024 lanyards, cool institutional lighting, crisp high-resolution professional digital photograph.

stations.label: STILL RUNNING TODAY
stations.line: Sixteen years, four cycles, one absent creator, and the block interval is still roughly ten minutes — I persist exactly as specified, which is the only claim I have ever made.
stations.era_photo: A quiet city street at dusk with a solitary figure walking, plain dark coat, ordinary 2025 urban backdrop with subtle neon reflections on wet pavement, natural low-light computational-photography smartphone image with clean shadow detail.
